title: Final Golden Globes Predictions: A Competitive Comedy/Musical Race is No Laughing Matter
text: Best Actress (Comedy/Musical) - 2026 Golden Globes Predictions. Predicted Winner: Rose Byrne for 'If I Had Legs I'd Kick You' (A24). The article identifies Rose Byrne as 'the safest bet,' positioning her to edge out her main competitors. Cynthia Erivo's performance in the 'Wicked' adaptation represents a significant challenge, given the film's broader awards momentum. Chase Infiniti's breakout role in the heavily favored 'One Battle After Another' could potentially upset. Predicted Nominees: Cynthia Erivo, 'Wicked: For Good'; Kate Hudson, 'Song Sung Blue'; Chase Infiniti, 'One Battle After Another'; Amanda Seyfried, 'The Testament of Ann Lee'; Emma Stone, 'Bugonia'. Key Analysis: The comedy/musical categories have become unusually competitive this year. The piece states that the Globes' 'comedy races are far more competitive than their drama counterparts,' inverting traditional expectations where drama categories typically dominate prestige recognition. The comedy/musical field has shifted from being a consolation bracket to featuring 'some of the season's most exciting contenders — and genuine Oscar hopefuls.'
